objects.name: Host Status
objects.description:
Logging, messaging, and other artifacts that highlight the health and operational state of host-based security sensors, such as Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agents, antivirus software, logging services, and system monitoring tools. Monitoring sensor health is essential for detecting misconfigurations, sensor failures, tampering, or deliberate security control evasion by adversaries.

*Data Collection Measures:*

- Windows Event Logs:
    - Event ID 1074 (System Shutdown): Detects unexpected system reboots/shutdowns.
    - Event ID 6006 (Event Log Stopped): Logs when Windows event logging is stopped.
    - Event ID 16 (Sysmon): Detects configuration state changes that may indicate log tampering.
    - Event ID 12 (Windows Defender Status Change) – Detects changes in Windows Defender state.
- Linux/macOS Monitoring:
    - `/var/log/syslog`, `/var/log/auth.log`, `/var/log/kern.log`
    - Journald (journalctl) for kernel and system alerts.
- Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) Tools:
    - Monitor agent health status, detect sensor tampering, and alert on missing telemetry.
- Mobile Threat Intelligence Logs:
    - Samsung Knox, SafetyNet, iOS Secure Enclave provide sensor health status for mobile endpoints.

x_mitre_log_sources.name: networkdevice:syslog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: no logging host, no aaa new-model, no snmp-server, commit

x_mitre_log_sources.name: android:appops
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION|NEARBY_DEVICES|BLUETOOTH_SCAN used in close proximity to network-context queries

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AndroidAttestation:SafetyNet
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: SafetyNet attestation with CTSProfileMatch=false or BasicIntegrity=false

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AndroidAttestation:VerifiedBoot
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Verified Boot or dm-verity reports partition hash mismatch, non-green boot state, or integrity failure

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AndroidLogs:Crash
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Crash or abnormal restart of privileged system services (for example, system_server, mediaserver, installd) followed shortly by new privileged process activity or binder connections from a single app UID

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AndroidLogs:Crash
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Application or system process crash/restart patterns temporally associated with remote service communications

x_mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: firmware_update, kexec_load

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudMetrics
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Autoscaling, memory/cpu alarms, or instance unhealthiness

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudWatch
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sustained spike in CPU usage on EC2 instance with web service role

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudWatch
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: StatusCheckFailed or StatusCheckFailed_System for burstable instances (t2/t3)

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudWatch
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sustained EC2 CPU usage above normal baseline

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudWatch
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: NetworkOut spike beyond baseline

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudWatch
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sudden spike in network output without a corresponding inbound request ratio

x_mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudWatch
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Unusual CPU burst or metric anomalies

x_mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:hostd
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Powering off or restarting host

x_mitre_log_sources.name: iOS:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Device risk, compliance, or security posture changes after trusted host pairing or developer-state transition

x_mitre_log_sources.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: code signature validation failure / exec of invalidly-signed payload from sandboxed app

x_mitre_log_sources.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Application crash logs, watchdog terminations, or abnormal execution events associated with service communication

x_mitre_log_sources.name: journald:boot
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Secure Boot failure, firmware version change

x_mitre_log_sources.name: kubernetes:events
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, container restart count exceeds threshold

x_mitre_log_sources.name: linux:procfs
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sustained high /proc/[pid]/stat usage

x_mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Out of memory killer invoked or kernel panic entries

x_mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Service stop or disable messages for security tools not reflected in SIEM alerts

x_mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: system is powering down

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:osquery
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: interface_details

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:syslog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Hardware UUID or device list drift

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Web service process (e.g., httpd) entering crash loop or consuming excessive CPU

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Spike in CPU or memory use from non-user-initiated processes

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Termination or disabling of XProtect, Gatekeeper, or third-party AV daemons

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: network stack resource exhaustion, tcp_accept queue overflow, repeated resets

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: EFI firmware integrity check failed

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: System Integrity Protection (SIP) state reported as disabled

x_mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: System shutdown or reboot requested

x_mitre_log_sources.name: MDM:DeviceIntegrity
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: jailbreak/root compromise indicators or integrity attestation failures enabling process visibility

x_mitre_log_sources.name: networkdevice:syslog
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: System reboot scheduled or performed

x_mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: TCP: possible SYN flood or backlog limit exceeded

x_mitre_log_sources.name: OEMAttestation:Knox
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Samsung Knox attestation shows attestation_state=COMPROMISED or warranty bit set

x_mitre_log_sources.name: prometheus:metrics
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Container CPU/Memory usage exceeding threshold

x_mitre_log_sources.name: sar:network
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Outbound network saturation with minimal process activity

x_mitre_log_sources.name: Sensor Health
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: None

x_mitre_log_sources.name: Windows:perfmon
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sustained CPU/memory exhaustion by service process (e.g., w3wp.exe)

x_mitre_log_sources.name: Windows:perfmon
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: High sustained CPU usage by a single process

x_mitre_log_sources.name: Windows:perfmon
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sudden spike in outbound throughput without corresponding inbound traffic

x_mitre_log_sources.name: Windows:perfmon
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Sudden spikes in CPU/Memory usage linked to specific application processes

x_mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-TCPIP
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: Connection queue overflow or failure to allocate TCP state object

x_mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:Security
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: EventCode=1166, 7045

x_mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:Security
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: EventCode=1074

x_mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:Security
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: EventCode=6006

x_mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:Sysmon
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: EventCode=16

x_mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:System
x_mitre_log_sources.channel: System shutdowns due to bugcheck (Event ID 1001) or watchdog timer expirations
